SEARCH

Class BinaryTree<E>

java.lang.Object
BinaryTree<E>
Type Parameters:
E - the type of elements stored in the tree
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tree<E>
Direct Known Subclasses:
BinarySearchTree
public abstract class BinaryTree<E> extends Object implements Tree<E>
Generic abstract binary tree implementing the Tree interface. insert() is left abstract since ordering is not defined here.
Version:
4/21/26
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

    The root node of the tree.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    contains(E e)
    Checks if the tree contains the element by checking every node.
    void
    empty()
    Clears the tree by setting root to null.
    String
    inOrderString()
    Returns an inorder traversal string: left then root then right.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if the tree has no elements.
    String
    postOrderString()
    Returns a postorder traversal string: left then right then root.
    String
    preOrderString()
    Returns a preorder traversal string: root then left then right.

      The root node of the tree.

  • Constructor Details

    • BinaryTree

      public BinaryTree()
      Constructs an empty BinaryTree.

  • Method Details

    • contains

      public boolean contains(E e)
      Checks if the tree contains the element by checking every node. Uses a recursive helper since there is no ordering guarantee.
      Specified by:
      contains in interface Tree<E>
      Parameters:
      e - the element to search for
      Returns:
      true if the element is found

    • preOrderString

      public String preOrderString()
      Returns a preorder traversal string: root then left then right.
      Specified by:
      preOrderString in interface Tree<E>
      Returns:
      preorder string

    • postOrderString

      public String postOrderString()
      Returns a postorder traversal string: left then right then root.
      Specified by:
      postOrderString in interface Tree<E>
      Returns:
      postorder string

    • inOrderString

      public String inOrderString()
      Returns an inorder traversal string: left then root then right.
      Specified by:
      inOrderString in interface Tree<E>
      Returns:
      inorder string

    • empty

      public void empty()
      Clears the tree by setting root to null.
      Specified by:
      empty in interface Tree<E>

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if the tree has no elements.
      Specified by:
      isEmpty in interface Tree<E>
      Returns:
      true if root is null
